results.editorial:
PH Guardian Senior Care Centre sits at No. 1, Jalan Putra Bistari 2/1T in Putra Heights, a residential township in the southern Subang Jaya corridor. It operates as a private nursing home serving the growing elderly population in this area, which sits roughly between Subang Jaya and Puchong. The centre's stated aim is compassionate, personalised care with dignity and respect at the centre of everything — standard positioning, but the Putra Heights address gives it good proximity to both Subang Jaya Medical Centre and Putrajaya Hospital.

PH Guardian advertises 24-hour care with qualified nurses and caregivers, fully furnished air-conditioned rooms, WiFi, Astro, hot-water showers, handrails, bath chairs, and round-the-clock CCTV monitoring. It is listed as handling mild dementia cases, suggesting nursing dependency is catered for, though the extent of medical complexity they accept is not publicly detailed. Pricing starts from RM2,800 per month with no registration fee, which is broadly in line with mid-tier Subang Jaya nursing homes.

The centre has only three Google reviews (5-star average), which makes independent assessment difficult. No Facebook activity, website content beyond the basic pages, or any JKM licence number was found publicly. Given the small footprint of online presence, families should visit in person, clarify what nursing qualifications are held by on-duty staff, confirm whether a doctor makes regular rounds, and ask specifically what level of medical dependency — PEG feeding, tracheostomy, oxygen — they can or cannot manage.

facts.red_flags: Very thin online presence — only 3 reviews, no licence number found, no detail on nursing qualifications or clinical capability ceiling

results.editorial:
Home Sweet Home Care Centre operates from No. 43, Jalan Gasing in Bukit Gasing, Petaling Jaya — a location that gives it good access to Pantai Hospital about 4 km away and University Malaya Medical Centre roughly 5 km to the north. The centre is led by Managing Director Dr. Paul Anthony Fredericks, a distinguishing credential that signals a medically-oriented approach rather than a purely social-care model. It is a multi-branch operation — a second location runs at Jalan SS2/32 in Sea Park — so the Jalan Gasing site handles a specific catchment rather than being the sole centre.

The clinical service list is substantive: 24-hour nursing care, in-house doctor for minor ailments and minor procedures, in-house nutritionist, physiotherapy, wound dressing, NGT and PEG tube management, catheter care, blood sampling, and vital signs monitoring. Care programmes include post-stroke rehabilitation, palliative care, Parkinson's management, and dementia support. Residents receive five meals daily and have access to Netflix and WiFi. The combination of on-site doctor availability and tube-feeding competency puts this above a basic custodial home.

Capacity figures are not published, and pricing is not listed publicly — families should enquire directly. The three Google reviews at 5 stars provide minimal signal on consistent quality. Worth asking: what is the nurse-to-resident ratio on night shift, how often does the in-house doctor attend each resident (rather than being called on demand), and whether the Jalan Gasing branch has the same staffing depth as the SS2 branch.

facts.red_flags: Only 3 reviews; capacity and pricing not disclosed publicly; multi-branch — confirm Jalan Gasing has same staffing as SS2 branch

results.skip_reason: No website, no Facebook, no discoverable details beyond name, phone number, and Google Maps pin in SS14 Subang Jaya. No third-party listings, no reviews with substantive content, no mention in any Subang Jaya nursing home directory.
results.editorial:
Elderly Care Centre is listed under a Subang Jaya address in the SS14 area with a phone number (+60 18-383 7704) but has no website, no Facebook presence, and no coverage in any nursing home directory or review platform. Only a Google Maps pin with three brief 4.7-star reviews was found, without substantive content about services or facilities. There is insufficient verifiable information available online to write a meaningful profile for this facility.

Families researching this centre would need to call directly to establish what care types are offered, whether it holds a JKM licence, how many residents it accommodates, the nursing staff qualifications, and what medical complexity it can handle. SS14 is a mature residential neighbourhood in Subang Jaya with reasonable proximity to Subang Jaya Medical Centre.

Given the near-total absence of public information, this listing should remain as a stub until direct contact or a site visit can verify the basics — care types, licence status, staffing model, and capacity. Families should not shortlist this centre without first speaking to the operator.

facts.red_flags: No online presence whatsoever — no website, no Facebook, no directory listings; unable to verify even basic care type or licence status

results.skip_reason: Minimal verifiable information. Only a Waze/Google Maps entry placing it at 142 Jalan Bukit, Kajang with a landline number (+60 3-6737 3571). No website, no Facebook, no directory listings with substantive detail.
results.editorial:
Kajang Nursing Home appears at 142 Jalan Bukit in Kajang town proper — a central Kajang address that gives it reasonable proximity to Hospital Kajang (under 3 km) and KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital. A landline number (+60 3-6737 3571) is the only reliable contact detail found. No website, Facebook page, or substantive directory listing was discovered during research. The Google Maps entry yields three reviews at a 3.7-star average, suggesting some residents or family members have used it, but none of the reviews contained usable detail about services or conditions.

The name "Kajang Nursing Home" implies a nursing-level rather than purely social-care operation, but without a website or JKM licence number it is not possible to verify staffing model, capacity, clinical capability, or how long it has been operating. The central Kajang Jaya township location in a residential area is typical of smaller, community-embedded homes that may serve long-term Kajang residents who prefer staying local.

Families considering this home should call ahead to establish whether it is currently accepting residents, confirm licence status with JKM Selangor, ask about nursing coverage overnight, and visit in person before committing. The below-average rating warrants direct investigation into what specific concerns reviewers had.

facts.red_flags: 3.7-star rating with no explanatory detail; no website or Facebook; no JKM licence confirmed; below-average rating should be explored directly

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Barakah is based in Saujana Rawang, a growing township north of Rawang town proper in Selangor. It positions itself as an Islamic-oriented full-care centre for elderly Muslims, with a brand identity built around compassion and family-like care. The facility operates its own website (wargaemasbarakah.com, though the domain was redirecting at time of research) and has a Facebook presence, suggesting it is actively marketing. Monthly fees start from RM1,900, which is at the lower end for Selangor private nursing homes and likely reflects a shared-room model.

Based on available information, Barakah provides full-day and residential care, nutritious meals prepared according to halalan toyyiban standards, and spiritual programming including Quranic reading sessions, religious lectures, and group dhikr. A team of experienced doctors is cited as part of the medical support structure, though the frequency of doctor visits versus on-demand access is unclear. Activity programming includes gardening, art therapy, and light exercise. The Rawang location means the nearest major hospital is likely Hospital Rawang or facilities in Sg. Buloh, so families with medically complex residents should factor in transport time.

Pricing from RM1,900 is attractive, but families should clarify what that includes — whether meals, diapers, and medications are bundled or billed separately. Confirm nursing qualifications of day and night staff, the centre's JKM registration status, and whether they can handle higher-dependency residents (bedridden, PEG-fed, or needing wound care). The low Google review count (3 reviews at 3.7 stars) limits confidence without a direct visit.

facts.red_flags: 3.7-star rating; website domain was redirecting at research time; pricing from RM1,900 — confirm what is included; remote Rawang location adds hospital access risk for complex cases

results.editorial:
Noble Care Retirement Resort occupies units SCS G1–G6 within Country Heights Villas in Kajang — a gated township that lends the property an upscale residential character quite different from a typical nursing home. Noble Care is a multi-site operator with branches in Cheras, Ampang, Johor Bahru, and USJ, giving it more than two decades of cumulative experience across the group. The Country Heights location is marketed as a resort-style retirement option with a treehouse, gym, zen gardens, rooftop terrace, spa, sauna, Yoga, and Tai Chi, which positions it firmly at the premium leisure-retirement end of the spectrum rather than as a high-dependency medical facility.

That said, Noble Care's service sheet does include clinical capabilities: palliative care, dementia care and treatment, respite care, stroke and cancer care, bedsore dressing, Foley catheter management, nasogastric tube changes, colostomy bag changes, tracheostomy care, and physiotherapy. This suggests the Country Heights site can manage nursing-dependent residents beyond purely ambulant retirees, though the resort framing suggests this is not the primary market. The facility is within roughly 20 minutes of Serdang Hospital, KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital, and Hospital Kajang.

No public pricing was found — expect a call-to-enquire model typical of premium operators. With only two Google reviews (both 5-star), independent assessment is very limited. Families should clarify whether the nursing and medical capabilities are fully available on-site at Country Heights specifically, or whether those services are primarily delivered at other Noble Care branches. Also ask about nurse-to-resident ratios overnight, given the resort emphasis on lifestyle rather than clinical depth.

facts.red_flags: Only 2 reviews; pricing not public; verify that full clinical services (palliative, dementia, tracheostomy) are delivered at Country Heights specifically and not just at other branches

results.skip_reason: No website, no Facebook page found, no directory listings with any substantive details. Name does not appear in any Klang nursing home roundup or third-party listing. Only Google Maps pin in Taman Sri Andalas, Klang with a contact number (+60 13-879 4006) and 2 reviews at 5 stars.
results.editorial:
Athena Care Centre is pinned to Taman Sri Andalas, a township in the southern Klang area with reasonable proximity to Hospital Tengku Ampuan Rahimah — the main public hospital for Klang — roughly 8–10 km away. Despite an active Google Maps listing with phone contact, no website, Facebook page, or appearance in any nursing home directory or editorial roundup was found during research. Two reviews at 5 stars provide essentially no usable signal about services, staffing, or quality.

Without any public information about what care types Athena offers, whether it holds a JKM licence, how many residents it accommodates, or what nursing capability is on duty, it is not possible to write a meaningful profile. Taman Sri Andalas is a mixed residential-commercial area that could support a small community nursing home, but the near-complete absence of digital presence makes verification impossible without a direct visit or phone call.

Families should call the listed number to confirm the facility is operating, establish care type and licence status, and arrange a site visit before considering placement. The Klang area has several well-established and more easily verifiable alternatives for comparison.

facts.red_flags: No web or social presence whatsoever; not found in any directory; cannot verify licence, services, or staffing; 2 reviews only

results.skip_reason: No website, no Facebook page found under this exact name. Google Maps pin places it in Taman Nirwana, Ampang area with 2 five-star reviews. Care type is listed as charity, suggesting a welfare/donation-funded model, but no substantive public information found.
results.editorial:
Pusat Penjagaan Warga Emas Hati Mesra appears in Taman Nirwana, which falls within the broader Ampang corridor in Selangor — an area with access to AMPANG Puteri Specialist Hospital and Hospital Ampang. The centre is categorised as a charity operation, consistent with donation-funded Islamic welfare homes (rumah kebajikan) that typically serve lower-income elderly residents who may have limited family support. No phone number is listed in the data, and no website or Facebook page was found under this exact name during research.

The two five-star Google reviews suggest some positive user experiences, but the absence of any contact pathway or public information makes this facility nearly impossible for families to independently assess or contact. A care type of "charity" in the Malaysian context typically implies means-tested admission, donor-funded operations, and care provided largely by volunteers or welfare officers rather than registered nurses — though some charity homes do employ qualified staff.

Families interested in this type of welfare-oriented home should contact JKM Selangor directly to confirm the centre's registration status, eligibility criteria, and current operational status. Placement in a charity home usually requires a social welfare referral rather than a private admission process, and capacity is often limited.

facts.religion: Islam (likely — Taman Nirwana area, welfare home naming convention)
facts.red_flags: No phone number or contact path found; no website or social media; charity model may have means-tested or referral-only admission; cannot verify operational status

results.editorial:
Mega Nursing Home is located at No. 1, Jalan 14/47A in Taman Aman, Petaling Jaya — a mature residential neighbourhood close to the Federal Highway, about 3 km from Pantai Hospital and accessible to SJMC. The facility is classified as both a nursing home and assisted living facility, indicating it caters to a spectrum from ambulant residents needing support through to fully dependent cases. Its description emphasises a home-like environment with a serene courtyard, wet-room ensuite bedrooms, and a large multi-functional room — language that prioritises comfort without claiming a resort finish.

Mega Nursing Home provides 24-hour specialist care, with access to trained staff covering nursing, pharmacy, and physician services along with on-site diagnostic capabilities. Residents include individuals living with dementia, diabetes, hypertension, and depression, and the facility is reported to care for residents aged over 100 years — suggesting comfort with long-term, complex chronic care. Religious, laundry, and housekeeping services are included, and a daily activities calendar covers exercise and recreational programming. A second phone number (+60 12-777 5582) appears in some directories alongside the number in our data.

Only two Google reviews (5 stars) are available, which limits independent verification. Pricing is not publicly listed. Families should clarify what "physician services" means in practice — whether a doctor is on-site daily or visits periodically — and confirm the extent of dementia-specific capabilities, including whether they have a designated dementia unit or manage it in a mixed-ward setting. Asking about night nursing ratios and escalation protocols will help gauge how they handle medical emergencies.

facts.red_flags: Only 2 reviews; pricing not public; confirm whether physician is on-site daily vs periodic visits; clarify dementia care model

results.skip_reason: Very limited public information. Facebook page exists (Sunshine Nursing Care Centre) but content is minimal. Located in Bukit Sentosa, Rawang — a northern Selangor residential area. Only 2 reviews at 3-star average. Pricing reportedly from RM50/day including diapers and 24-hour care per Facebook listing but no further detail verified.
results.editorial:
Sunshine Nursing Home Care is listed in Bukit Sentosa, an outer residential area in the Rawang district of northern Selangor. The centre has a Facebook presence but limited content, and online information is sparse. A pricing mention of RM50 per day (approximately RM1,500/month) including diapers and 24-hour care was found in a directory listing attributed to their Facebook page — this would position it at the budget end of the market, though this figure should be confirmed directly as it may reflect an older rate or a basic shared-room package.

The Bukit Sentosa location is relatively remote — the nearest major hospital is likely Hospital Rawang or facilities in Sg. Buloh, and access to specialist hospitals requires a longer drive into the Klang Valley. For medically stable, low-dependency elderly residents whose families live in the Rawang-Bukit Sentosa corridor, this proximity can be an advantage in terms of family visiting. The two Google reviews at a 3-star average are concerning and warrant direct investigation into what drove the lower ratings.

Families should call the listed number (+60 12-474 6615), visit in person, and specifically ask about nursing staff qualifications and overnight coverage. At budget pricing points, the model often leans on trained caregivers rather than registered nurses, which may be acceptable for ambulant or minimally dependent residents but not for those with complex medical needs. Confirm JKM licence status and what clinical situations would necessitate hospital transfer.

facts.red_flags: 3-star rating (only 2 reviews); remote Bukit Sentosa location increases hospital transfer time; thin online presence; budget pricing (~RM50/day) may indicate caregiver-only staffing model rather than nursing
